search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae minor head injury Issue 2 signs assessment red flags investigations observation referral
candidate_sources_rejected: CT, observation-duration, analgesia and discharge instructions
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Risk classification and diagnosis require clinician assessment.
No investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, observation duration or instruction is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with possible minor head injury; anticoagulation, infancy, older age, seizure, penetrating injury and polytrauma require additional assessment.
setting_applicability: DHA virtual-care domains partially transfer to UAE emergency in-person assessment.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA evidence; UAE trauma, imaging, neurosurgery, pediatric, safeguarding and observation pathways govern.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 (2024) exact source was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No later official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA section supports onset, mechanism, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context, vital data, focused examination and appearance only when assessed; it does not classify injury severity automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports recording time-critical symptoms only when clinician-assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigations and referral section supports recording existing imaging or tests, escalation and specialist discussion only when performed or decided.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-determined CT, MRI, or X-ray documentation and immediate referral or escalation when serious-injury criteria are present.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact observation section supports recording a clinician plan and arranged handover or follow-up only when completed; no duration or instruction is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented observation, head-injury advice, analgesia decisions, worsening-symptom return precautions, and arranged follow-up without fixed routine timing or doses.

unresolved_source_gaps: Loss of consciousness, amnesia, vomiting, seizure, anticoagulants, intoxication, Glasgow Coma Scale, skull signs, neurological detail, CT criteria, diagnosis, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
